description: Post-v8 code quality: split remaining oversized page-level functions (Analytics 221 lines, Dashboard, JobBoard, etc.), extension error handling hardening (unhandled promise rejections, MSG constant consistency, shared constants), and minor backend cleanups.

userStories.title: Split Analytics.jsx page component (221-line function)
userStories.description: frontend/src/pages/Analytics.jsx has a 221-line main function. Extract chart sections into sub-components (e.g., AnalyticsCharts.jsx, AnalyticsSummaryCards.jsx) and data processing into a useAnalyticsData hook. The page component should compose these pieces.
acceptanceCriteria: No function in Analytics.jsx or extracted files exceeds 40 lines
All files stay under 300 lines
npm test passes all 430+ tests
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Largest remaining function in the entire frontend.

userStories.title: Split Dashboard.jsx, JobBoard.jsx, Register.jsx, Login.jsx page functions over 40 lines
userStories.description: Multiple page components have functions exceeding 40 lines: Dashboard.jsx, JobBoard.jsx, Register.jsx, Login.jsx. Extract sub-components and hooks from each to bring all functions under 40 lines.
acceptanceCriteria: No function in Dashboard.jsx, JobBoard.jsx, Register.jsx, or Login.jsx exceeds 40 lines
All files stay under 300 lines
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Page-level components that were missed in prd_8 (which focused on components/).

userStories.title: Split remaining component functions: DetailPanel, FilterBar, NavBar, ContactForm (40+ lines each)
userStories.description: These components still have functions exceeding 40 lines after prd_8: DetailPanel.jsx (152-line fn), FilterBar.jsx (149-line fn), NavBar.jsx (141-line fn), ContactForm.jsx (141-line fn). Extract sub-components and form logic into hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: No function in DetailPanel.jsx, FilterBar.jsx, NavBar.jsx, or ContactForm.jsx exceeds 40 lines
All existing tests pass
npm test passes all tests

userStories.title: Extension: add try/catch to all async addEventListener callbacks
userStories.description: Multiple async addEventListener callbacks lack error handling, causing silent promise rejections: content.js line 71 (handleSave click), contact_banner.js line 26 (handleContactSave click), popup.js line 179 (storage.local.set). Wrap each in try/catch with console.error logging and user-visible fallback where appropriate.
acceptanceCriteria: Every async addEventListener callback in content.js, contact_banner.js, and popup.js has try/catch
Caught errors are logged via console.error with descriptive prefix (e.g., '[content] Save failed:')
npm test in extension/ passes all tests
userStories.notes: Unhandled rejections can silently break extension features.

userStories.title: Extension: unify MSG constants into shared module
userStories.description: MSG constants are duplicated and inconsistent across extension files. contact_banner.js uses a bare string constant MSG_SAVE_CONTACT instead of the MSG object pattern. PAGE_TEXT_MAX_CHARS is 3200 in content.js but hardcoded in background.js:126. MAX_RECENT is 5 in popup.js but hardcoded in background.js:106. BANNER_SUCCESS_DISMISS_MS is in banner_helpers.js but hardcoded 1500 in contact_banner.js:71. Create a shared extension/shared/constants.js with all MSG types and shared constants, import everywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: A single extension/shared/constants.js (or similar) defines all MSG types and shared numeric constants
contact_banner.js uses MSG.SAVE_CONTACT from the shared module, not a bare string constant
background.js references PAGE_TEXT_MAX_CHARS and MAX_RECENT from shared constants, no hardcoded duplicates
contact_banner.js uses BANNER_SUCCESS_DISMISS_MS from banner_helpers.js or shared constants
popup.js MS_PER_DAY (86400000) is a named constant
npm test in extension/ passes all tests
userStories.notes: Duplicate constants are a maintenance hazard.

userStories.title: Extension: add error logging to content.js silent catch blocks
userStories.description: content.js has empty catch blocks that silently swallow errors: line 145 (storage read) returns false without logging, and other catch blocks lack context. Add console.error with '[content]' prefix to all catch blocks. Also add console.error logging to popup.js:173 which has a bare 'catch { return; }'.
acceptanceCriteria: No empty catch blocks in content.js or popup.js — all include console.error with descriptive message
Error messages include '[content]' or '[popup]' prefix for debugging
npm test in extension/ passes all tests

userStories.title: Extension: remove unused MSG.GET_RECENT_SAVES from popup.js and clean up unused exports
userStories.description: popup.js line 19 defines MSG.GET_RECENT_SAVES but never uses it (recent saves are read from chrome.storage.local directly). background.js:178 exports handleSave but it's a service worker that doesn't use ES module imports in the main path. background.js defines MSG.AUTH_STATUS but never uses it. Clean up these dead definitions.
acceptanceCriteria: MSG.GET_RECENT_SAVES removed from popup.js (or used if routing was intended)
Unused MSG.AUTH_STATUS removed from background.js
No orphaned exports in background.js
npm test in extension/ passes all tests

userStories.title: Backend: optimize sequential awaits in seo/router.py with asyncio.gather()
userStories.description: seo/router.py lines 80-81 execute two independent database queries sequentially: job_lastmod = await _get_latest_job_lastmod() and slugs = await _get_active_share_slugs(). These should use asyncio.gather() for parallel execution.
acceptanceCriteria: seo/router.py uses asyncio.gather() for the two independent queries
Behavior unchanged — same data returned
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Only sequential await violation found in the backend.

userStories.title: Backend: split remaining 7 functions over 40 lines
userStories.description: Seven backend functions still exceed 40 lines after prd_8: fit_scorer.py score_fit() 49 lines, main.py create_app() 47 lines, applications/router.py list_applications() 45 lines, documents/service.py update_document() 43 lines, auth/router.py refresh() 42 lines, auth/oauth_service.py get_or_create_github_user() 42 lines, auth/email_verification.py resend_verification() 41 lines. Split each into smaller helpers.
acceptanceCriteria: No function in any of the listed files exceeds 40 lines
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
Type annotations on all new functions
userStories.notes: All are marginal violations (41-49 lines). create_app() may be acceptable as app factory.

userStories.title: Frontend: add aria-hidden to CsvImportModal overlay div
userStories.description: CsvImportModal.jsx has a backdrop overlay div that should have aria-hidden='true' for screen reader accessibility (similar to how CommandPalette.jsx handles its overlay).
acceptanceCriteria: CsvImportModal overlay div includes aria-hidden='true'
npm test passes all tests

userStories.title: Split remaining page components with functions over 40 lines: Settings, Calendar, ForgotPassword, ResetPassword
userStories.description: Additional page components likely have functions over 40 lines that were not covered in previous stories. Check Settings.jsx, Calendar.jsx, ForgotPassword.jsx, ResetPassword.jsx and any other pages/ files. Split oversized functions into sub-components and hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: No function in any pages/*.jsx file exceeds 40 lines
All files stay under 300 lines
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Catch-all for remaining page-level function splits.
